name: The White House
subject: Steps Toward a Denuclearized North Korea
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bad668f1f71e3b32a13ac6bdc1b38b75c741777ddb54237dbd6bfef3db30dcf4ed90718b77fce52131a8c35d8fdf5d138b88cef9b302dc62

President Trump Meets with Cabinet

Yesterday, President Donald J. Trump met with his Cabinet to discuss a range of pressing domestic and foreign policy issues. The President instituted a crucial step toward denuclearizing the Korean Peninsula by designating North Korea as a state sponsor of terrorism, announcing that the Treasury Department will be imposing “the highest level of sanctions” on Pyongyang over a two-week period.

The Cabinet meeting also focused on a range of domestic policy issues, including the historic tax cuts bill passed by the House of Representatives last week. “We’re going to give the American people a huge tax cut for Christmas,” said President Trump. “Hopefully that will be a great, big, beautiful Christmas present.”

If the bill becomes law, the United States would go from being “one of the highest-taxed nations in the world to one of the lowest-taxed.” Tax reform comes amid the Administration’s continued efforts to reduce government spending. “We’ll be submitting plans on health care, plans on infrastructure, and plans on welfare reform – which is desperately needed in our country – soon after taxes,” President Trump said.

Trump Administration Hard at Work on 'the Crisis Next Door'

New data compiled by the Council of Economic Advisers (CEA) reveals the opioid crisis in the United States is more severe than previously thought. Opioid-involved overdose deaths doubled in the past 10 years and quadrupled in the past 16 years, and drug overdoses are now the leading cause of death in the United States, outnumbering traffic crashes or gun-related deaths. President Trump has committed to deploying the tools of government to confront this intolerable epidemic head-on.

Learn more about the problem and the Trump Administration’s plan of attack
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bad668f1f71e3b328dc66528b050532becc476d8853560372a346ee687c3e8a0ce9bab2592f1d6b513d46dea9e411d763e0c031959ca56d5
here.

The White House Christmas Tree Has Arrived!

Yesterday afternoon, serenaded by Christmas carols, the National Christmas Tree Association presented the White House Christmas Tree to First Lady Melania Trump and Barron Trump at the North Portico. This year’s Christmas tree is a 19.5-foot balsam fir selected by White House Chief Usher Timothy Harleth. The tree was grown at Silent Night Evergreens in Endeavor, Wisconsin, and will reside in the Blue Room for the 2017 holiday season.

See highlights from the presentation
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bad668f1f71e3b32e251a8704d4e6750357b8393701966cba5efe429418cb558f5a9b40a6263b68a7be4d3d74986179f8e809e8ce9d5c158
here.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump holds a cabinet meeting | November 20, 2017 (Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Trump will continue a White House Thanksgiving tradition by pardoning the National Thanksgiving Turkey in a ceremony in the Rose Garden. The two turkeys chosen to come to Washington are fittingly named Drumstick and Wishbone. This year’s ceremony marks the 70th anniversary of the National Thanksgiving Turkey presentation, which began with President Harry Truman in 1947.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bad668f1f71e3b32c6077c850c1036c245d65bd63c68468ec5091d240982f3dd7cf3f82182bb51dcfb76580ec50fcba53fb44e3d13e79d43
Watch the pardoning live at 1pm ET.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=bad668f1f71e3b32acda71ce3f0111d58353ee64b051ced37ec6a75121a2eb55071fe91b302a7055b46b97de4c2cba42ed5cdcfb14123293
Privacy Policy |

Contact the White House |

name: DNC War Room
subject: ✍️ Tell Senate Republicans: Stop scamming middle-class Americans
body:
Demand a fair tax plan from the GOP.
---
**Add your name to demand a tax plan from Senate Republicans that works for every American:

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Defeat-the-GOP-Tax-Plan1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20171121_dnc_nd&source=em_20171121_dnc_nd")**
Derek --

A quick note on the Senate GOP's tax bill:

Beyond the fact that this plan heavily favors the richest 1% and corporations, the Senate version of the bill will have debilitating effects on our economy, our debt, and deepening income inequality.

Get this: According to the nonpartisan Joint Committee on Taxation, within a decade the Senate's tax plan will increase taxes on most people making under $75,000, Derek.

This scam of a bill doesn't help regular people now, and it sure won't help us later.

We have to be vigilant, Derek. We have some time, but not much before the Senate votes on their version of the tax bill (as soon as next week). **So add your name today to let Senate Republicans know that we demand a tax plan that works for everyone, not just those at the very, very top:**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Defeat-the-GOP-Tax-Plan1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20171121_dnc_nd&source=em_20171121_dnc_nd")**

Add your name to demand a tax plan from Senate Republicans that works for every American: "" Derek -- A quick note on the Senate GOP's tax bill: Beyond the fact that this plan heavily favors the richest 1% and corporations, the Senate version of the bill will have debilitating effects on our economy, our debt, and deepening income inequality. Get this: According to the nonpartisan Joint Committee on Taxation, within a decade the Senate's tax plan will increase taxes on most people making under $75,000, Derek. This scam of a bill doesn't help regular people now, and it sure won't help us later. We have to be vigilant, Derek. We have some time, but not much before the Senate votes on their version of the tax bill (as soon as next week). So add your name today to let Senate Republicans know that we demand a tax plan that works for everyone, not just those at the very, very top: "" Thanks, The Democrats Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

name: Jay Inslee
subject: The global leadership we need
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
By working together with Canada, we'll fight climate change.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTI0MTA1MDIS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTk5MDYxNAS2&mt=1&rt=0) Friend, You and I know the White House is diminishing America's role as a global leader. But while President Trump sits, I'll stand tall. It's why I went to Bonn. It's why I'm building a movement for climate action in Washington state, up and down the West Coast, and across America. And it's why I'm in British Columbia today. Today in Vancouver, I have the honor of addressing the Legislative Assembly of British Columbia for a simple and critical reason: Now, more than ever, climate action must be a global movement, and it requires leadership at the local, state, and international level. This work takes a commitment from all of us. We all need to invest in putting a better foot forward for climate leadership in Washington state and America. [**So I 'm asking: Show you're with us too by chipping in $15, one dollar for every member of the U.S. Climate Alliance.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTI0MTA1MDIS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTk5MDYxNAS2&mt=1&rt=0) Countries across the world are hungry to work with America to combat climate change -- and with a lack of leadership in D.C. they're turning to Washington state to collaborate on climate action. Working closely with Canada will be essential to slowing the progress of climate change. I plan to continue the work from Bonn, Germany last week, when our U.S. Climate Alliance joined with leaders from Mexico and Canada to create the North American Climate Leadership Dialogue. I look forward to what we'll accomplish. Together, the Dialogue will tackle everything from clean transportation to clean technology, from carbon pricing initiatives to climate pollutants. We're together in this. Trump doesn't believe that the hurricanes, floods, and forest fires we've seen in 2017 have anything to do with climate change, but he is wrong. That's why we will do everything we can to make a difference locally, nationally, and globally. [**Leadership starts at home, and so I am asking for your help to show the strength of our climate movement -- chip in $15 today.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTI0MTA1MDIS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTk5MDYxNAS2&mt=1&rt=0) Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [CONTRIBUTE »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTI0MTA1MDIS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTk5MDYxNAS2&mt=1&rt=0)
---

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: FWD: together we can defeat this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| Thanksgiving is around the corner, which means we'll have the opportunity to gather and share conversations with those who mean the most to us. While many families have an understandable aversion to discussing politics around the table, this year the stakes are especially high, and if the discussion does turn that way, it's important to be ready with facts.

Since the Republican tax plan impacts everyone and would be very damaging to millions of middle class families if it passes, I want to make sure you saw my message from over the weekend detailing the threat this bill poses. [**Please sign here to stand against this bill and read my message below for more details >> **](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bb8/70cdc2ba/46ccd511/3902e8c/2168780404/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/stop-republican-giveaways?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Please+sign+here+to+stand+against+this+b&utm_campaign=em112117&source=em112117")

_Begin forwarded message:_

> What is happening is totally outrageous.
>
> This week, Republicans in the House of Representatives passed a tax bill that would dramatically increase income inequality in America by giving the already wealthy a big tax cut that is financed by increasing taxes on millions of middle class families, leaving our children and grandchildren to pay-off a whopping $1.5 trillion increase in the national debt.
>
> So it's hard to believe that the Republicans on the Senate Finance Committee could pass something even worse -- but they did. First, they eliminated entirely the ability for people to deduct their state and local taxes. As a result, millions of middle class families will see their taxes go up to pay for tax giveaways to big corporations. Second, they repealed the provision in the Affordable Care Act that requires everyone to take some responsibility for their own health care coverage. Eliminating that provision will spike premiums by more than 10% and result in 13 million fewer Americans with affordable health coverage.
>
> [**We cannot afford another "faith-based" trickle-down bill built around wishful thinking that a massive giveaway to large corporations and the wealthiest will eventually help everyone else. I will be doing everything I possibly can to stop this tax bill, but I need you to be engaged in this battle too. Sign here to stand with me against this outrageous bill. >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bb8/70cdc2ba/46ccd511/3902e8d/2168780404/VEsE/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/stop-republican-giveaways?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+We+cannot+afford+another+faithbased+tric&utm_campaign=em112117&source=em112117")
>
> The truth is that there are many common sense measures we could take to reform our tax code in a bipartisan way. But Republicans in Congress are not interested in talking. They want to ram through their corporate giveaways before the public has time to see what's in their bill.
>
> I know tax policy can be dry at times, but we can't afford to sit out this debate. These Republican tax plans, if passed, will do severe damage to our country for decades to come.
>
> [**We defeated the effort to destroy the ACA because people rose up in protest. We need you again. Please reach out to people you know around the country and urge them to get engaged in this fight. clean_body: | Thanksgiving is around the corner, which means we'll have the opportunity to gather and share conversations with those who mean the most to us. While many families have an understandable aversion to discussing politics around the table, this year the stakes are especially high, and if the discussion does turn that way, it's important to be ready with facts. Since the Republican tax plan impacts everyone and would be very damaging to millions of middle class families if it passes, I want to make sure you saw my message from over the weekend detailing the threat this bill poses. "" Begin forwarded message: What is happening is totally outrageous. This week, Republicans in the House of Representatives passed a tax bill that would dramatically increase income inequality in America by giving the already wealthy a big tax cut that is financed by increasing taxes on millions of middle class families, leaving our children and grandchildren to pay-off a whopping $1.5 trillion increase in the national debt. So it's hard to believe that the Republicans on the Senate Finance Committee could pass something even worse -- but they did. First, they eliminated entirely the ability for people to deduct their state and local taxes. As a result, millions of middle class families will see their taxes go up to pay for tax giveaways to big corporations. Second, they repealed the provision in the Affordable Care Act that requires everyone to take some responsibility for their own health care coverage. Eliminating that provision will spike premiums by more than 10% and result in 13 million fewer Americans with affordable health coverage. "" The truth is that there are many common sense measures we could take to reform our tax code in a bipartisan way. But Republicans in Congress are not interested in talking. They want to ram through their corporate giveaways before the public has time to see what's in their bill. I know tax policy can be dry at times, but we can't afford to sit out this debate. These Republican tax plans, if passed, will do severe damage to our country for decades to come. "" This is likely to come down to one vote in the Senate. If we all stand together, we can defeat this bill. Thank you for staying engaged in the fight! name: Jill Donnelly
subject: What we're thankful for
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
Our children, our time together, and you, Peter. |
---

| | Peter,

Joe and I are thankful to have our children home for Thanksgiving. We look forward to sharing a turkey dinner, watching football, and putting up Christmas lights, but we look forward to being together most of all.

As we enjoy dinner, I'd imagine that our conversation will include national and international topics as well as Joe's 2018 re-election bid for the United States Senate. We're less than a year away from Election Day, and Joe's not worried about the polls or the giant target that the special interests will put on his back.

But that's the thing about Joe. He's simply thankful for the opportunity to represent Hoosier families in the Senate, and he's excited to continue working for them. While the Koch brothers and other groups are already pouring money into this race to frame him as something he's not, he just wants to make sure that Hoosiers have a voice.

**Peter, Joe will never stop fighting for Hoosiers. But he can 't win this race alone. Joe and I would be very appreciative for whatever you could chip in before the holiday festivities begin. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2526/7377f838/358b3a49/51fa5fcc/1653741763/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20171121nd&amount=10")**

As we gather around the table on Thursday, Joe and I we will be thinking about you. We are so thankful that you're in his corner. It's for folks like you that he gets up every day ready to fight for Indiana.

**Thank you again for standing with Joe and for helping us meet our end-of-month goal to keep Joe 's common sense voice in the Senate.**

Best,
Jill

name: =?utf-8?Q?Eric=20Greitens?=
subject: Fighting for our kids and teachers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
body:
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Peter,
Let me tell you why we're fighting so hard for kids and teachers in Missouri.

Today, the system works for insiders and bureaucrats who get paid real well, but it fails too many students, families, and teachers.

We know insiders and bureaucrats will lie. You deserve to know the facts.

Here's a lie: Missourians don't give enough tax money to schools.

Here are the facts: Missourians spend about the national average on our schools. I support public schools, and our team supports public schools. We made education a priority. We put more money into schools than ever before in Missouri history. We fully funded K-12 education for the first time in years, and we added $64.6 million to the K-12 budget.

Where did that money go? Bureaucrats took it.

In our schools, we've got too many bureaucrats. We're top ten in the country for our number of school administrators. And they're well paid. In fact, in Missouri, administrator pay has been increasing more than twice as fast as teacher pay. Several administrators make more than $250,000.00 a year. Six figures.

That money should go into the classroom. It should go to teachers.

Our teachers deserve to get paid more. Missouri is ranked 40th in the country in teacher pay. Our pay for starting teachers: 48th in the country. Third worst in the nation.

Our teachers deserve better. Our students also deserve better.

Here's a lie: The bureaucrats say our schools are doing just fine, that there's no need for change.

Here are the facts: We need to support teachers, because our schools have been getting worse. From 2009 to 2015, Missouri fell from 18th to 28th in fourth-grade reading and from 23rd to 32nd in eighth-grade math.

In fact, even if they graduate, according to ACT testing, three out of every four Missouri kids aren't fully ready for college. Education Week gave Missouri a D+ for K-12 achievement.

This year, we put more money into education than ever before--but the bureaucrats cut the ACT for high schoolers.

These aren't opinions. They are facts.

Schools should serve kids. Our kids and families deserve better. They deserve the truth.

Here's why bureaucrats are lying: they're desperate to prevent change. If things change and people start asking hard questions, they will have to provide clear answers.

Question: This year, Missouri's schools got more money than ever before, so how come teachers didn't get a bigger raise? Where did the money go?

Question: How come our administrator pay has been rising more than twice as fast as teacher pay?

These are the facts. These are the questions that need to be asked.

There are a lot of people committed to the status quo. They've been willing to harass and intimidate anyone who stands up to them. That won't stop us from doing what's right. We're fighting to get results for Missouri teachers and students.

Thank you,
Eric Greitens

Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
This email was sent to dpwillis67+greitens@gmail.com. You can also follow us on [Facebook](https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=17ee10c2d6&e=84041254e6) and [Twitter](https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=e68e896035&e=84041254e6). name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Devastating
body:
#
Hello,

Heat waves, rising sea levels, and dangerous extreme weather conditions -- these are just a few of the impacts of climate change that the Clean Power Plan aims to reduce.

I know that clean energy is our future. Right here in Tallahassee, we're putting clean energy first with our new 120-acre solar farm that will triple our solar energy capacity.

But right now, the Trump Administration wants to roll back the clock and undo the progress not only our country -- but also our city -- has made. And if they succeed, it will be devastating. Just a few days ago we saw this impact when the Keystone pipeline ruptured -- and yet Nebraska still voted to move forward with the Keystone XL pipeline.

We've got to do better friend, so I need your help:

**[We have until December 15 to overwhelm the EPA with feedback to protect the Clean Power Plan during the public comment period. Will you add your name and join me and other elected offices in making our voices heard?](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3252/9e00690/4ec7edc4/24030844/1523763174/VEsH/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/clean-power-plan/gillum/?utm_campaign=em17_171121_ClimJA_acq_gq_ful__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em17_171121_ClimJA_acq_gq_ful__AG&utm_content=1+-+We+have+until+December+15+to+overwhelm+t")**

It's simple: climate change is here -- and we need to band together to combat it.

Thankfully, several dozen cities in our state have committed to moving toward 100 percent clean and renewable energy. And more than 100 mayors have signed onto an initiative to support 100 percent clean energy, myself included.

Listen, we've only got one planet to call home, and we need everyone's help to fight back and make sure we embrace a plan that transitions our state (and country) to clean energy as rapidly as possible.

We have a chance to start the change right now. Protecting the Clean Power Plan is my commitment as a public servant and citizen -- and not an election year promise.

**[If you're with me, add your name and then submit an official public comment -- we have to act immediately.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3252/9e00690/4ec7edc4/24030847/1523763174/VEsE/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/clean-power-plan/gillum/?utm_campaign=em17_171121_ClimJA_acq_gq_ful__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em17_171121_ClimJA_acq_gq_ful__AG&utm_content=2+-+If+youre+with+me+add+your+name+and+then+")**

Andrew

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3252/9e00690/4ec7edc4/24030846/1523763174/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=3+-+receive+fewer+emails&utm_campaign=em17_171121_ClimJA_acq_gq_ful__AG"). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3252/9e00690/4ec7edc4/24030859/1523763174/VEsC/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=4+-+unsubscribe&utm_campaign=em17_171121_ClimJA_acq_gq_ful__AG&source=em17_171121_ClimJA_acq_gq_ful__AG"). [You can support our campaign by making a donation.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3252/9e00690/4ec7edc4/24030858/1523763174/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-footer?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=5+-+You+can+support+our+campaign+by+making+a&utm_campaign=em17_171121_ClimJA_acq_gq_ful__AG&source=em17_171121_ClimJA_acq_gq_ful__AG")
